steps.title: Identify the perimeter of the rectangle
steps.content: A rectangle has four edges: two lengths of $x$ feet each and two widths of $y$ feet each. Fencing along each edge means covering the entire perimeter: $x + x + y + y = 2x + 2y$ feet.

steps.title: Set the perimeter equal to the total fencing
steps.content:
Hassan used 44 feet of fencing, so:
$$2x + 2y = 44$$

steps.title: Evaluate each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$2x+2y=44$': The perimeter of a rectangle is twice the length plus twice the width, which equals 44. ✓
  • Choice B — '$x+y=44$': This only sums one length and one width — it forgets that a rectangle has two of each. ✗
  • Choice C — '$2xy=44$': This involves a product ($xy$), which gives area, not perimeter. ✗
  • Choice D — '$xy=44$': $xy$ is the area formula, not the perimeter. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
